description: When an ExternalTwoByteString that serves as the parent of a SlicedString is internalized via String::MakeThin(), it can be converted into a ThinString pointing to a SeqOneByteString (when the equivalent internalized string has one-byte encoding). However, the SlicedString's own instance type (kTwoByteStringTag | kSlicedStringTag) is never updated to reflect this encoding change. In JsonStringifier::SerializeString(), after calling String::Flatten() on the SlicedString (which returns the SlicedString unchanged, since SlicedStrings are considered flat), the code checks IsOneByteRepresentation() on the SlicedString's stale instance type, concludes it is two-byte, and calls SerializeString_<uc16, uc16>. That function calls GetCharVector<uc16>() which invokes GetFlatContent(), follows the parent chain (SlicedString -> ThinString -> SeqOneByteString), and obtains a one-byte FlatContent. The subsequent DCHECK(flat.IsTwoByte()) fires in debug/ASAN builds; in non-ASAN release builds the one-byte buffer is read as two-byte data, causing an out-of-bounds read and garbled output.

fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6304799 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
